detailed_analysis: agentic_tool_spec.py is the Layer-3 port (architecture §4, §5.1) defining the seam between use cases and tool adapters. It is a passive descriptor: six frozen dataclasses/Protocols (FileLayout, SingleFileLayout, RulesFileLayout, DirectorySkillLayout, SharedKeyedMapLayout, CustomizationTypeIO, AgenticToolSpec), one predicate (is_reserved_customization_name), and one registry factory (default_agentic_tools). Code health is strong: immutable value objects, fail-fast validation in three __post_init__ hooks (storage/layout coherence, fixed_file_name suffix invariant, capability-matrix drift detection), zero hardcoded secrets, zero injection vectors, no global mutable state, no deep nesting, and no concrete tool-name branching inside the type definitions (the only tool names are confined to the default_agentic_tools registry wiring, which is the permitted location per architecture §1). The one genuine code-quality breach is the module length: 310 lines exceeds the project's explicit 300-line module limit (AGENTS.md §3); architecture §4 itself records the file as '~310', so this is a known, marginal overrun rather than uncontrolled growth. Remaining observations are minor: storage shape strings ('single_file', 'directory_skill', 'shared_keyed_map') are repeated as inline literals across layout classes and the §5.1 contract without a shared named constant (mild magic-string / DRY smell), and the CustomizationTypeIO function-type fields rely on module-level Callable aliases without runtime validation (acceptable for a frozen descriptor). No CRITICAL or MAJOR findings. Spec/architecture conformance is clean: the file satisfies US-10 AC-1, AC-5, AC-6 and architecture invariants for goal 5 / NFR-11.

dependency_graph_summary.notes: Lazy imports only: tool_specs factories imported inside default_agentic_tools() (line 291-299) and get_format imported inside SharedKeyedMapLayout.file_suffix (line 165). Both are deferred to call time, avoiding import cycles and import side effects. No top-level intra-package imports — Dependency Rule (architecture §3) upheld; this Layer-3 port references no use-case (Layer 2) or framework (Layer 4) module.

highest_fan_in.imported_by: use cases sync/adoption/discovery/tool_status + rendering + all tool_specs factories (high blast radius)

findings.evidence: File spans lines 1-310; AGENTS.md §3 sets the module limit at 300 lines. architecture.md §4 records the file as '~310'.
findings.problem: Module is 310 lines, 10 over the project's explicit 300-line limit (AGENTS.md §3). This is marginal and already acknowledged in architecture §4 ('~310'). Not a God Module: the file holds a single cohesive concern (the port's value objects + the registry factory) with high cohesion and no unrelated responsibility clusters. Flagged only because it crosses the hard numeric limit the project sets for itself.

The storage-shape discriminator strings 'single_file', 'directory_skill', and 'shared_keyed_map' are repeated as inline literals in each layout class and described again in the architecture §5.1 contract. They are load-bearing identity tokens compared elsewhere (CustomizationTypeIO.storage validation, line 211) yet are not centralised as named constants or an enum. A drift between a layout's literal and a consumer's expected literal would not be caught at construction. Low blast radius given the frozen-dataclass design, but a named constant set would make the contract explicit.

findings.evidence:
parse: ParseFn
    render: RenderFn
    extract_pair_id: ExtractPairIdFn
findings.problem: The three function fields are typed via module-level Callable/Protocol aliases (lines 22-36) but are never validated as callable at construction time, unlike the storage/file_layout/fixed_file_name invariants which ARE enforced in __post_init__. This is consistent with the project's 'validate at boundaries, trust inside' rule (AGENTS.md §8) since the registry factories are the construction boundary, so this is an observation rather than a defect. No action implied beyond awareness.

spec_adherence.notes: US-10 AC-1 (single-module spec surface): the AgenticToolSpec/CustomizationTypeIO contract and default_agentic_tools registry satisfy the structure. AC-5 (unique name by construction): registry is a dict keyed by name (lines 301-309) — duplicate keys structurally impossible, no separate dedup step. AC-6 (fail-closed construction): AgenticToolSpec.__post_init__ raises ValueError naming the tool and divergent customization_types (lines 257-266); CustomizationTypeIO.__post_init__ raises when neither storage nor file_layout is coherent (lines 203-216). AC-7/AC-8 are owned by config/registry-build, not this file. Architecture goal 5 / §1 'sync engine never references a concrete tool name': the type definitions are tool-agnostic; the only tool names ('antigravity', 'claude', ...) appear in default_agentic_tools registry wiring (lines 302-308), which §1 explicitly permits as the adapter/registry layer. NFR-11 (extensibility): adding a customization_type/tool needs only a new file_layout class + factory, matching the requirement text. Dependency Rule (§3) upheld via lazy imports (lines 165, 291). No NFR-15 secret-egress concern: this is a passive descriptor with no egress boundary. No deviations found.

verdict_summary: Clean tool-agnostic port; only defect is a 10-line module-length overrun (310 vs 300) already noted in architecture §4. No CRITICAL/MAJOR findings; full US-10 / goal-5 / NFR-11 conformance.
